quoted_tweet.text:
Tesla has launched a new Model 3 lineup in Canada, introducing the Model 3 Premium RWD starting at $39,490 CAD (that converts to just $29,000 USD!).

Tesla has also cut the Model 3 Performance price by 17% to $74,990 CAD (from $89,990).

The Model 3 Premium RWD trim is about 50% cheaper than the previously lowest-priced Model 3 Long Range ($79,990 CAD) in Canada, which has just been discontinued. The price to get into a Tesla just got a lot more affordable in Canada!

kept.text: True, but I hate monthly subs. Tesla should allow annual subscriptions.

profile_bio.description: We have fun here

quoted_tweet.text:
I just witnessed a Launch Edition Model Y get rear-ended, hard enough that it’s likely headed for a total loss. 😢

What makes it especially painful is that the Launch Edition came with lifetime FSD baked into the purchase price at a significant premium. Tesla made that FSD license non-transferable. So if the car is written off, the lifetime FSD dies with it. All that extra money paid upfront vanishes along with the vehicle.

This is exactly why the FSD subscription model usually makes more sense for most buyers. You pay monthly only while you own and drive the car. If it gets totaled or traded in, you simply cancel the subscription. No massive sunk cost disappears with the vehicle.

It’s a reminder that hardware is replaceable, but those non-transferable software licenses aren’t.

kept.text:
STARLINK IS FUNDING HUMANITY’S FUTURE ON MARS

While most people see Starlink as “just satellite internet,” 
Elon has been clear about its deeper purpose.

“Starlink is how we are paying for humanity to get to Mars.”

This is why the Starlink router even has a Mars transfer orbit diagram on it.

Every new user, every subscription, and every expansion of the network directly helps fund Starship development — the vehicle that will eventually carry people and cargo to Mars.

Starlink isn’t just connecting the world today.
It’s helping build the multiplanetary future tomorrow.
The revenue from nearly 10,000 satellites and 9+ million users is fueling the biggest engineering project of our time.
This is one of the most elegant loops in tech history: global connectivity today, interplanetary civilization tomorrow.

kept.text:
Having a balanced and diversified portfolio like this in the coming months will let you ride out the volatility, while enjoying the upside momentum.

Overexposure will only make you emotional and reactive.

These are my picks below:

profile_bio.description: Ideas are free. Understanding takes Cryptic Trades.

quoted_tweet.text:
The stocks you must own in the coming months:

Defensive (30-35% of Portfolio Exposure)

- Treasury Bonds: $TLT, $TLH, $SHY
- Consumer Staples: $TGT, $UPS, $KO
- Healthcare: $LLY, $UNH, $OSCR, $HIMS
- Consumer Discretionary: $LULU, $BABA, $JD, $UBER

Cash (5-10% of Portfolio Exposure)

Growth/Risk Assets (65-70%)

- Tech: $TSLA, $ORCL, $SNOW, $MSFT 
- FinTech: $HOOD, $COIN, $PYPL 
- Bitcoin Proxies: $IREN, $CIFR, $MSTR 

By having a diversified portfolio like this, you'll be able to ride out the volatility on the short-term, while taking advantage of the long-term uptrend.

All of these stocks are also in high-timeframe accumulation ranges and still undervalued on the long-run, unlike most stocks that already rallied and will cool off in the near-term.

profile_bio.description: Ideas are free. Understanding takes Cryptic Trades.

kept.text:
🚨 This headline is straight-up misleading clickbait.
“Elon Musk was paid $158 billion by Tesla last year.”
Wrong. Tesla’s own SEC filings show that’s just the grant-date accounting fair value of unvested performance-based stock awards.
Elon’s actual realized compensation in 2025? $0. Zero salary, zero cash bonus. The performance milestones weren’t met, so no payout.
This isn’t a paycheck — it’s skin in the game. He only gets rewarded if Tesla creates trillions in value for all of us shareholders.
Comparing a paper accounting number to the entire Dept of Education budget? Peak propaganda.

profile_bio.description: مخالف هرنوع حاکمیت مادام العمر، فامیلی، اسپرمی

kept.text:
When I started publishing in 2018, after hesitating for 5 years, predicting that BEVs will conquer the world and explained why this was not ecologically but economically driven, the outcry, hatred, and resistance of my articles were unprecedented.

The process that started with the IPO of Tesla accelerates not because all of a sudden interest to reduce CO2 emissions but because the gap between the total cost of ownership of a BEV and an ICE car is so big that even the strongest haters change their mind.

We are still in the early days, but companies like Tesla will be the biggest beneficiaries of this change, while the established car manufacturers who refused to listen and change will slowly but surely disappear.

The irony of history is that the biggest supporters of the fossil fuel industry – be it Trump, the MAGA movement and Russia, to name just a few – have achieved with their actions  the opposite of what they wanted.
